exemplar.title: Do high school students above 16 years old recall more content if they handwrite their notes or type them?

detailedExemplarData.title: Do high school students above 16 years old recall more content if they handwrite their notes or type them?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: The aim and relevance are clearly stated and linked to realâworld academic benefit.
Independent and dependent variables are operationalized, with both null and directional hypotheses articulated.
Levelsâofâprocessing theory is described and connected to the investigationâs predictions.
weaknesses: Minor inaccuracies in the description of levelsâofâprocessing stages create some conceptual confusion.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The introduction meets almost all topâband descriptorsâaim and relevance are well explained, variables are fully operationalized, and hypotheses are clearly stated. The only notable shortcoming lies in slight misrepresentations within the theory description. Overall performance aligns with a solid midâtop band placement; scaled against the total mark constraint, a score of 3/6 reflects its relative strength among the criteria.

strengths: Independentâsamples research design is not only described but its choice is explicitly justified in terms of isolating group differences.
Opportunity sampling and participant characteristics are detailed, with rationale to mitigate bias.
Controlled variables and materials are named and briefly explained to maintain internal validity.
weaknesses: Random assignment procedures are not fully elaborated, weakening sampling validity.
The connection between specific controls and potential confounding variables could be more fully developed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration section demonstrates a wellâjustified design and decent sampling rationale, with explanations of materials and controls. However, depth is moderate in the latter two strands, placing it in the upper middle band (3â4/4). When rescaled to the overall total, a score of 2/4 appropriately acknowledges solid but improvable performance.

strengths: Appropriate descriptive statistics and MannâWhitney U test are applied.
Graphs (boxplot and bar chart) correctly present data and address the hypothesis.
Statistical findings are interpreted with regard to the data and linked to the hypothesis.
weaknesses: Minor rounding and reporting issues (e.g., unclear alpha selection, missing effect size) affect reproducibility.
Some graph elements lack full labels or unit annotations.
Calculation steps for the Uâvalue ratio are omitted, limiting transparency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis quality is high, with accurate application of statistical tests and clear graphical presentation addressing the hypothesis. Small technical and reporting errors prevent a perfect mark but justify placement in the 5â6/6 band. Scaled to fit the total mark, a score of 3/6 reflects strong overall performance with room for better reporting and detail.

strengths: Strengths and limitations are identified, explained, and directly tied to the investigationâs validity concerns.
Modifications are explicitly linked to stated limitations and well justified.
weaknesses: Discussion of findings remains somewhat superficial; deeper links to levelsâofâprocessing theory are needed.
Procedural descriptions and future implications are brief and lack detail.
The breadth of suggested future research and recommendation is limited.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation shows clear identification and explanation of strengths, limitations, and linked modifications, placing it in the 5â6/6 band for one strand and 3â4/6 for two strands. Overall, performance is solidly midâband. When adjusted to respect the totalâmark constraint, a score of 2/6 appropriately reflects its moderate strength relative to the other criteria.

strandSelections.reasoning: Boxplot and bar graph are appropriate, correctly depict the IV levels and DV means, include axes and directly address the hypothesis.  Although labels are brief, no notable technical errors are present, matching the top band.

The student states statistical results, interprets the p-value, explicitly accepts the null hypothesis, and discusses what the stats mean for the data pattern.  Interpretation is clearly linked to the hypothesis and data, fulfilling the highest descriptor.

Both IV (note-taking method: hand vs computer) and DV (score out of 10 on a recall quiz) are clearly stated and operationalised; directional and null hypotheses are articulated correctly.  This meets the highest descriptor with no evident omissions.

Opportunity sampling is named and demographic characteristics, inclusion criteria, and rationale (non-psychology seniors to avoid bias) are discussed, showing explanation of choice, though depth is moderate.

Levels-of-processing theory is described (shallow vs deep processing, elaborative rehearsal) and explicitly connected to the predicted superiority of hand-written notes.  While the structural breakdown of the theory is slightly inaccurate, the overall description and link to the investigation are sound and fulfil the top-band requirement.

Findings are compared with Mueller & Oppenheimer and linked back to levels-of-processing, but the discussion remains brief and somewhat surface-level; nonetheless, there is clear reference to theory, meeting the 3-4 band.

The aim â to test whether hand-written or typed notes improve recall â is clearly stated and the relevance is explicitly explained in terms of helping students choose an effective study method.  The justification links the classroom context and future academic benefit, satisfying the descriptor for an explained relevance.

Multiple methodological and sample-related strengths and limitations are identified and explained in relation to validity, boredom, prior knowledge, etc., demonstrating relevance and depth consistent with the top band.

Key controls (same instructions, video, quiz) and material selection are stated with brief reasoning (maintain consistency, avoid boredom).  This constitutes an explanation but with limited detail, meriting the 3-4 band.

Two concrete modifications (free-response test format, randomised sampling) are proposed and each is explicitly tied to a stated limitation.  Justification is present but concise, aligning with the 3-4 descriptor.
The student not only describes using an independent-samples design but also explains why it was chosen for detecting differences between two groups, fulfilling the âexplainedâ level.

Descriptive statistics and an appropriate Mann-Whitney U test for two independent, small samples are applied.  Calculations appear mostly correct; minor mean/SD rounding issues do not materially affect accuracy, so the strand fits âappropriateâ¦ but may contain errors.â

annotations.comment: Graph section header is truncated; ensure full descriptive titles so readers understand the figure without cross-referencing.

The description of levels-of-processing theory includes inaccuracies in the classification of processing stages; the link between shallow and structural processing is confusing. Clarify definitions and ensure accurate representation of Craik and Lockhartâs model.
The aim and relevance are clearly stated and linked to academic benefit, demonstrating strong context and justification for the study.
Independent and dependent variables are clearly operationalized with note-taking method and quiz score, meeting the highest criterion for hypothesis structure.
Including both null and directional hypotheses strengthens testability and clarity of expected outcomes.
The directional alternative hypothesis is articulated correctly, clearly predicting greater recall with handwritten notes.
The rationale for using an independent-samples design is well explained in terms of isolating group differences and maintaining consistent operational definitions.
Opportunity sampling and participant demographics are described and linked to mitigating bias, showing sound sampling justification.
Consider elaborating on how random assignment was implemented in opportunity sampling to strengthen the explanation of sampling validity.
The described controls on instructions, video, and test format support internal validity. Explain how these controls mitigate specific confounding variables.
The video selection is well justified to balance engagement and prior familiarity, enhancing ecological validity.
Table 1 header lacks a label for the first column, which hinders clarity. Add a descriptive header to indicate variable names.
The boxplot is appropriate but missing axis labels and units; include clear axis titles and scale markers.
Interpretation of overlap and âextentâ of data spread is vague; use interquartile range or variability measures to describe distribution quantitatively.
The bar graph effectively visualizes mean recall differences and addresses the hypothesis directly.
Reporting the ratio 1.00233 without showing the calculation limits reproducibility; include steps or formula used to derive this value.
Clarify the selection of critical U = 34 and whether this is one- or two-tailed; discuss alpha level and rationale for choosing Mann-Whitney U.
Include an effect size (e.g., r or eta-squared) to complement the non-significant p-value and contextualize the magnitude of the observed difference.
Discussion references Mueller & Oppenheimer and theory but remains superficial; deepen analysis by linking specific findings to levels-of-processing stages.
Explaining consistency in video and test use highlights strength of the independent design in controlling systematic variance.
Limitation of one groupâs prior performance is speculative; provide baseline equivalence data or pre-test results to substantiate this concern.
Sample demographic limitation is clearly stated; consider discussing how convenience sampling may affect external validity beyond size.
The final sentence of the procedure section is incomplete and unclear; revise to complete the thought and specify procedural strength or limitation.
Highlighting that multiple-choice questions may not elicit deep recall is insightful, demonstrating understanding of how measurement affects processing.
Modifications are well linked to limitations, justifying changes to test format and sampling to improve recall measurement and representativeness.
The conclusion accurately states no significance but should expand on implications for study strategy recommendations and future research directions.
